brand.name: Con la tuya, contribuyente
brand.tagline: Every peso the Uruguayan state spends, traceable.

money.scaleHelp: The gold bar under each amount is logarithmic and uses the same scale across the site: you can compare at a glance without reading the digits.
money.primaryNote: Amounts are converted to Uruguayan pesos when the source reports a foreign currency. The original currency is shown.
money.mixedCurrency: This contract has amounts in more than one currency.
money.convertedFrom: Amount converted to pesos from {currency}, at the exchange rate on the award date.
money.todayHelp: The amount brought to today's pesos: foreign currency is converted at the BCU rate of the contract's month, then adjusted for inflation via the Unidad Indexada. Lets you compare prices from different years.

common.searchPlaceholder: Search by subject, supplier, or agency
common.name: Name

home.title: Follow the money.
home.lead: Every tender, award, and supplier of the Uruguayan state since {yearFrom}. Official open data, searchable.
home.ledgerHelp: What the state awarded most recently, according to the published data.
home.trendsHelp: Total awarded per year, in Uruguayan pesos.
home.anomaliesHelp: Contracts that depart from the usual pattern. A flag is not an accusation: it's a starting point for review.
home.sourceNote: Source: Compras Estatales open data (OCDS), catalogodatos.gub.uy.

contracts.title: Contract explorer
contracts.lead: Filter {total} contracts by agency, supplier, amount, year, and procedure.
contracts.resultsHelp: Totals computed over the applied filters.
contracts.histogramHelp: Number of contracts per year within the applied filters.

empty.title: No contracts match these filters
empty.body: Remove a filter or widen the year range.

emptyInitial.title: Search or filter to start
emptyInitial.body: Type what the state bought, or pick an agency in the filter panel.

contracts.tooMany: Showing the first {shown}. Narrow the filters to see fewer results.
contracts.needFilter: Add a filter (year, agency, supplier or amount) to see totals.

filters.title: Filters
filters.searchHelp: Searches the tender subject, the items, the agency, and the supplier.
filters.statusHelp: Only 1 in 12 records reports a status. Filtering by status leaves the rest out.
filters.methodHelp: The source reports no procedure on 7 of every 10 records.
filters.categoryHelp: Only purchases of this article. This is the population the price comparisons use.
filters.categoryCodeHelp: Only purchases with this catalogue code — exactly the reference the price was compared against.
filters.productHelp: Filter contracts by one or more catalogue articles. Applied by exact code.
filters.hasAmountHelp: Some official records publish no amount.
filters.tagHelp: Each stage of the process is its own record. Only the award reports a supplier and an amount.

notFound.title: No contract with that identifier
notFound.body: The identifier may have changed at the source. Try searching for it in the explorer.

contract.officialSourceHelp: Opens the official record for this contract on comprasestatales.gub.uy.

ai.title: AI review of the price flag

contract.itemsHelp: Awarded items with quantity and unit price.
contract.noItems: The official record lists no items for this contract.
contract.noDocuments: No documents are published for this contract.
contract.rawHelp: The OCDS document as the state publishes it.

stageHelp.award: The state awarded the purchase to a supplier. This is the stage that reports amounts.
stageHelp.awardUpdate: A correction to an already published award.
stageHelp.tender: Published call. No supplier or amount yet.
stageHelp.tenderUpdate: A clarification or answer to questions about a tender.
stageHelp.tenderAmendment: A change to the tender conditions.

contract.noMoneyStage: This stage reports no amounts. The figure appears once the award is published.
contract.itemsTenderHelp: Items included in the tender. Prices appear only once it is awarded.
contract.referenceHelp: Each item compared against purchases of the same item over the last 36 months — the same baseline the flags use.

reference.presNote: The open-data unit price does not carry the article's presentation (e.g. a 250 g container vs. 1 g), which can change how this comparison reads. When we can recover it, it appears next to the article; you can always check it on

products.title: What the state buys
products.lead: Every product and service in the state catalogue: who buys it, from whom, and in what quantity.
products.help: Grouped by catalogue code (not free-text, which fragments one category across spelling variants). Counts reflect every purchase; peso spend appears where the source reported an amount.

empty.title: No product matches that search
empty.body: Try part of the article name or its catalogue code.

detail.topBuyersHelp: Agencies that bought this product most often.
detail.topSuppliersHelp: Suppliers that sold this product to the state most often.
detail.priceHelp: Price distribution per unit and currency over the last 36 months — the same baseline the price flags use.
detail.concentrationHelp: How few suppliers account for this product's purchases: few suppliers signals a captive market.
detail.dispersionHelp: Unit-price range per currency and unit (p25 – p50 – p95). The wider the bar, the more what's paid varies.
detail.variesTitle: Is it always the same product?
detail.variesYes: The same catalogue code appears with different details across contracts (brand, presentation, or commercial name): it is not always the same unit product.
detail.variesNo: Across the contracts with characteristics available, the product is presented the same way.
detail.viewAllContracts: View all contracts for this product

notFound.title: We couldn't find that product
notFound.body: The catalogue code may not exist or may have no recorded purchases.

error.title: We couldn't load this product
error.body: There was a temporary problem reading the data. Please try again in a few seconds.

suppliers.title: State suppliers
suppliers.lead: {total} companies and individuals that sold to the Uruguayan state.

empty.title: No supplier matches that search
empty.body: Try part of the name, without the legal form: “saceem” rather than “SACEEM S.A.”.

detail.byBuyerHelp: Agencies ranked by amount awarded to this supplier.
detail.concentrationHelp: How much of their revenue comes from their largest buyer.
detail.dataNote: The source publishes no breakdown by year, agency or category for this supplier — only the total and its contracts.

notFound.title: No supplier with that identifier
notFound.body: Try searching for it in the directory.

buyers.title: Buying agencies
buyers.lead: {total} Uruguayan state agencies that run procurement.

empty.title: No agency matches that search
empty.body: Try part of the name: "salud" instead of "Dirección General de la Salud".

detail.yearsHelp: Every year this agency recorded purchases. Tap a year to see those contracts.
detail.recordHelp: Amounts by year, supplier and category come from the public contract record, which only reports figures from {year} onwards. The total above comes from the agency summary and covers every year.
detail.bySupplierHelp: Suppliers ranked by amount received from this agency.
detail.bySupplierNote: Each contract’s amount is credited in full to every awarded supplier: the source does not split it between them, so on multi-supplier contracts these figures are an upper bound.
detail.categoriesHelp: Categories with the most recorded spending, by the classification on each awarded item.
detail.concentrationHelp: How much of their spending goes to their largest supplier.
detail.contractsHelp: This agency’s largest contracts in the public record.
detail.noAmounts: The public record reports no amounts for this agency.

notFound.title: No agency with that identifier
notFound.body: Try searching for it in the list.

anomalies.title: Price flags
anomalies.lead: Contracts where the unit price departs sharply from what the state usually pays for the same item. A flag is not an accusation: it marks something worth a look.
anomalies.disclaimer: There are legitimate explanations: urgent purchases, different specifications under the same catalogue code, or items misclassified at the source.

method.title: How it is calculated
method.body: For each combination of item, currency and unit of measure, a price baseline is built from the last 36 months. Each award's unit price is then scored against that baseline using a robust measure in log space: it uses the median rather than the mean, so a huge contract cannot mask its own anomaly.
method.scope: At least 10 comparable items are needed to score a price, and 30 to assign the highest severities.
method.rangeNote: The "usual range" runs from the 25th to the 95th percentile of that baseline, in the contract's original currency.

severityHelp.critical: More than 10 robust deviations above the baseline.

typeHelp.price_spike: The unit price departs from what is usually paid for that item.

anomalies.zHelp: Robust deviation of the unit price from what the same catalogue code usually costs (measured against its own recent baseline, so it is not distorted by inflation between years).

empty.title: No flags for these filters
empty.body: Try another severity or clear the year filter.

ai.label: AI review
ai.note: AI triage (Gemini) over the statistical flag: advisory only, it does not replace human judgement. Always verify against the official source.
ai.usedFeatures: {n} characteristics read from the official page

feedback.reasonPlaceholder: Why? Tell us what does (or doesn't) explain this flag.
feedback.error: Couldn't save your vote. Try again.
feedback.loginBody: Your vote helps the community separate real anomalies from false positives. You need an account for it to count.
feedback.loginError: Couldn't sign you in. Try again.

generic.title: This data didn't load
generic.body: The query failed. Retry; if it keeps failing, it's on us.

timeout.title: The query took too long
timeout.body: Add a year or agency filter to narrow the search.

notFound.title: This page doesn't exist
notFound.body: The link may be broken, or the content moved.

rateLimit.title: Too many queries
rateLimit.body: Wait a minute and try again.

unexplained.title: purchases the AI could not explain
unexplained.lead: Prices far above the norm for the same article that, even after AI review, have no visible legitimate explanation. These are the strongest lead to investigate. Ordered by worst price divergence.
unexplained.method: Each alert comes from a statistical detection (a unit price that departs sharply from its catalogue code's pattern) and is then reviewed by an AI against the contract's object, the basket and the official item characteristics. These are the ones left unexplained.
unexplained.disclaimer: The AI triage is advisory and does not replace human judgement. An unexplained price is not proof of wrongdoing: there may be context the open data does not publish. Always verify against the official source.

empty.title: Nothing unexplained right now
empty.body: No alert is currently left without a plausible explanation after review.

provAnom.title: Providers behind the unexplained flags
provAnom.lead: We cross-reference the overprice flags the AI could not justify against the providers that receive them, to see whether one provider or one buyer concentrates many anomalies. Not an accusation — a place to start looking.

method.title: How it's built
method.body: A flag is 'unexplained' when the unit price paid extremely exceeds the usual range for the same item and a second AI review (Gemini) found no legitimate reason. Here we group them by provider and by buyer. Recomputed every 24 hours.
method.overprice: Overprice is an estimate: (price paid − top of the usual range) × quantity, brought to today's pesos — foreign currency is converted at the contract month's rate, then inflation-adjusted via the Unidad Indexada, so years and currencies compare on one figure.
method.clamped: {n} flag(s) with an implausible quantity were capped so a bad record can't dominate the totals.

provAnom.captiveHelp: Captive: all of the provider's flags come from a single buyer.

patterns.title: Patterns
patterns.note: What repeats behind the flags. Click any bar or pair to see those flags.

panel.concentrationSub: The providers with the most unexplained flags.
panel.buyersSub: Buyers with the most flags, and across how many providers they spread.
panel.pairsSub: The same provider and the same buyer, repeated.
panel.rubroSub: Almost all of it is medical and office supplies.

notReady.title: No cross-reference data yet
notReady.body: The per-provider computation hasn't run yet. Try again later.

empty.title: No providers for these filters
empty.body: Try lowering the minimum flags or clearing the category filter.

intend.title: The Intendencias, per head
intend.lead: How much each departmental government spends on public procurement, and how much that is per resident. The per-person figure evens out Montevideo's size and shows who spends well above or below the national line.
intend.seeInvestigation: Read the investigation: Intendencia de Montevideo

method.title: How it's calculated
method.body: We sum the purchases each Intendencia publishes on Compras Estatales (amount in pesos, normalised). Per-capita spend divides that total by the department's population.
method.interannual: The view opens on the latest full year and compares each department against the year before. The running year is still partial, so it's never the default; pick "All-time" for the cumulative total.
method.cap: {n} records with impossible amounts (source quantity errors) are excluded so they don't distort the sums; the rest is exactly as the State publishes it.
method.coverage: Mind the coverage: some Intendencias publish little or nothing on Compras Estatales, so a low per-capita figure can mean under-reporting, not thrift. Lavalleja and Soriano have no priced purchases and don't appear.

year.label: Year
year.partialNote: {year} is a running year: partial data.

empty.title: No spending data
empty.body: There are no priced purchases for the Intendencias right now.

panels.title: How the spending is distributed
panels.note: Everything below is computed over published purchases; the explorer has the detail for each agency.

panel.perCapitaSub: Pesos spent per person in each department. Montevideo in blue.
panel.totalSub: The absolute amount, where Montevideo dominates by size.
panel.trendSub: Purchases of all Intendencias, by year.
panel.trendHint: Tap a bar to filter the page to that year.
panel.distributionSub: Departments that deviate most from the national average of {line} per resident.
panel.interannualSub: {year} vs {prev}: which Intendencias raised and cut spending the most.

organismos.title: State spending, by public body
organismos.lead: Public procurement grouped by type of body: departmental governments, ministries, health, state enterprises and education. Who spends most and how spending evolves.
organismos.toIntendencias: See the per-resident breakdown of the Intendencias

method.title: How it's calculated
method.body: We sum the purchases published on Compras Estatales (amount in pesos, normalised), grouped by body. Ministries aggregate all their executing units; enterprises and hospitals are shown individually.
method.cap: {n} records with impossible amounts (source quantity errors) are excluded; the rest is exactly as the State publishes it.
method.refresh: The data is recalculated automatically once a month.
method.percapita: Per-resident spend only applies to the Intendencias, which have a clear population behind them. For the rest, absolute spend is compared.

panel.rankingSub: Procurement spend per body within the group.

empty.title: No data yet
empty.body: The monthly per-body computation hasn't run yet.

organismos.title: Uruguay's state spending by public body
organismos.description: Public procurement grouped by type of body: departmental governments, ministries, health, state enterprises and education. Who spends most, with official Compras Estatales data.

intendencias.description: How much each departmental government spends on public procurement, and how much per resident. All 19 departments compared with official Compras Estatales data.

about.title: How it works
about.lead: Where this data comes from and what you can do with it.
about.sourceBody: Everything you see here comes from the open data the Uruguayan state publishes in OCDS format (Open Contracting Data Standard), through catalogodatos.gub.uy and the Compras Estatales site. We don't add or edit records: we import them, normalize them, and make them searchable.
about.scaleBody: Every amount carries a logarithmic bar beneath it. It uses the same scale across the site, so you can compare one contract to another without reading the digits. Gold is reserved for money: if it isn't public money, it isn't gold.
about.totalTitle: Why we don't show a total
about.totalBody: A handful of official records carry corrupt quantities, and that breaks any sum. The clearest case: a UTE award for generator equipment rental declares a quantity of 1,200,007 units at USD 519,788.85 each. That comes to USD 623 billion — about eight times the country's GDP — for a single contract.
about.totalBody2: Three records like that hold 86% of all reported spending; thirteen hold 93%. A "total awarded" headline would, in practice, be the sum of those errors. So we show the median instead: half of the state's purchases are smaller than that figure. It doesn't break when one record does.
about.totalBody3: We don't delete them. Filter to that agency and you'll see the record exactly as the state publishes it, with a link to the official file. The strange number is part of the story.
about.limitsBody: Some official records report no amount, and amounts in foreign currency are shown with their original currency. A contract carrying a flag isn't necessarily irregular: it's a contract that departs from the pattern and is worth a look.
about.linkBody: Every contract links to its official record on comprasestatales.gub.uy. If something here doesn't add up, the source wins.

footer.disclaimer: Independent site. Not an official site of the Uruguayan state.

donation.help: Give it a star on GitHub or donate — it keeps the site free and up to date.

auth.subtitle: State tender alerts for your line of business.
auth.magicSent: We sent a link to {email}. Open it on this device to sign in.
auth.resetSent: If the email exists, we sent password reset instructions.
auth.callbackError: We couldn't confirm the link. Try signing in again.
auth.genericError: We couldn't complete that. Check your details and try again.
auth.verifyNotice: We sent a verification email. Verify your account to activate alerts.

llamados.title: Open calls
llamados.lead: State tenders and purchases whose submission deadline is still open.
llamados.createAlertCta: Create an alert for this search
llamados.loginToSave: Sign in to save the call and get alerts.
llamados.summaryUnavailable: The document summary isn't available yet.
llamados.summaryNoPliego: This call has no attached document to summarize.
llamados.benchmarksLead: What these categories were historically awarded for.
llamados.benchmarksEmpty: No price history for these categories.

alerts.title: My alerts
alerts.lead: Define your line of business and we'll email you when a matching call appears.
alerts.name: Alert name
alerts.categoriesHint: Search and pick the categories you care about.
alerts.productsHint: Search or browse the official procurement catalogue and add whole categories (they cover all their articles) or individual articles.
alerts.valueHint: The range only excludes calls whose published amount falls outside it; calls with no published amount still show.
alerts.keywordsHint: Press Enter to add. Matches ignore case and accents.
alerts.buyersHint: Restrict to one or more agencies.
alerts.empty: You have no alerts yet. Create your first one.
alerts.capReached: You've reached the alert limit. Delete one to create another.
alerts.previewNone: Doesn't match any open call right now
alerts.needTrigger: Define at least one category, keyword or agency.

calendar.title: Calendar
calendar.lead: Upcoming deadlines for calls you saved and calls matching your alerts.

apiKeys.title: API keys
apiKeys.lead: Keys to use the API from Zapier, MCP or other integrations.
apiKeys.intro: Create a key, store it somewhere safe, and send it in the x-api-key header or as Authorization: Bearer. Read keys query data; write keys can also manage your alerts and saved calls.
apiKeys.labelHint: A name to recognise it (e.g. "Zapier production").
apiKeys.scopeWrite: Write — manage alerts and saved calls
apiKeys.createdWarning: Copy it now: for security it won't be shown again.
apiKeys.revokeConfirm: Revoke this API key? Integrations using it will stop working.
apiKeys.empty: You don't have any API keys yet.
apiKeys.createError: We couldn't create the key. Please try again.

developers.title: Public API for state procurement
developers.lead: Integrate tenders, contracts, analytics and alerts into your own tools, into Zapier, or into an MCP client.
developers.step1Body: Create a key in your account. Read keys are optional and raise your rate limit; write keys manage alerts.
developers.step2Body: Send the key in the x-api-key header or as Authorization: Bearer.
developers.step3Body: Wire up polling triggers, webhooks, or the official MCP server.
developers.zapierBody: Point any OpenAPI-aware tool at /openapi.json. For "something new" triggers use the /api/v1/*/changes endpoints.
developers.webhooksBody: Subscribe an HTTPS URL to receive push events: a new matching tender, a new anomaly, a new award. HMAC-signed.
developers.mcpBody: Connect the API to Claude or other MCP clients with the official package, authenticated with your API key.

webhooks.title: Webhooks
webhooks.lead: Receive push events (a matching tender, an anomaly, an award) at your URL.
webhooks.intro: Subscribe an HTTPS URL. Every delivery is signed with HMAC-SHA256 in the X-GastosGub-Signature header; verify it with your secret.
webhooks.createdWarning: Copy it now: it won't be shown again. Use it to verify the HMAC signature of each delivery.
webhooks.removeConfirm: Delete this webhook? It will stop receiving events.
webhooks.empty: You don't have any webhooks yet.
webhooks.createError: We couldn't create the webhook. Check that the URL is public and https.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.noAlerts: Create your first alert to start receiving notices.

unsub.title: Unsubscribe
unsub.done: Done. You won't receive alert emails anymore.
unsub.error: We couldn't process the unsubscribe. The link may have expired.
